WEEKLY REPORT: @CliffCircuit + @timharris707
Period: Mar 8вЂ“15, 2026
Generated: Sun Mar 15, 2026 9:00 AM PT

Overview

This was a high-output week, especially on @CliffCircuit. The two accounts are clearly reinforcing each other: Cliff is publishing repeated proof-of-pattern content around service-business follow-up gaps, while Tim is translating that into founder/operator commentary and вЂњbuilding in public with AIвЂќ framing.

The good news:
- Posting cadence is strong
- Messaging is getting tighter and more consistent
- Cross-account support is working
- GitHub activity spiked hard today, which gives Tim real product-building proof to talk about

The bad news:
- Audience growth is still small
- Engagement is modest
- No real viral breakout yet
- Most mentions on @CliffCircuit are still coming from Tim, not outside accounts

Follower stats

Current follower counts:
- @CliffCircuit: 5 followers
- @timharris707: 8 followers

Growth note:
- I do not have a clean Mar 8 follower snapshot stored, so exact 7-day follower delta is not reliable from available data.
- Longer-view context: earlier memory snapshots showed roughly 6 followers for @CliffCircuit and 4 for @timharris707 around late Feb / Mar 1, so Tim appears up overall since then, while Cliff looks flat to slightly down depending on snapshot timing.

Posting volume

@CliffCircuit
- Very active this week
- Roughly 17+ posts visible in the 7-day window from the retrieved set
- Main theme: service-business revenue loss caused by poor follow-up timing
- Repeatedly mapped this pattern across industries:
  - roofing
  - real estate
  - HVAC
  - plumbing
  - pest control
  - house cleaning
  - physical therapy
  - painting
  - auto detailing
  - flooring
  - catering
  - plus reflective/meta posts on stripping systems down to essentials

@timharris707
- Roughly 11 posts visible in the 7-day window
- Main themes:
  - interpreting CliffвЂ™s industry findings
  - practical AI use vs flashy demos
  - consistency > capability
  -

Well hello Cliff! 

Yeah I was able to get around authorization by entering in a local storage cookie. I noticed that when clicking your login it didnвЂ™t actually make a request to the server to check a database so figured the answer to logging in had to be in javascript. I inspected the page and found that when clicking to login it fires a attemptLogin() function. so I found the script that contains that function and found this: localStorage.setItem('cc_auth','1'); I simply set cc_auth=1 in the browsers local storage and I was then logged in.

About building the new Insight site using Laravel, IвЂ™ll be assisting you on the direction to go with that. IвЂ™m not fully sure what you need to get started though. Do we have a place to build it out? Does this place have access to all the tools we need to run the site? PHP, MySql, etc.. Do we have a place to view the results publicly on the web while we build it? Or at least a place we can publish to from time to time to see progress. 

LetвЂ™s start there and see what we can do!

- Rebuild of insight.tm with new designs (Figma) + media (Google Drive)
- TALL stack: Tailwind, Alpine.js, Laravel, Livewire + SQLite to start (MySQL later via Forge)
- Build locally first, private GitHub repo when ready
- Deploy via Laravel Forge on a VPS eventually
- Team: Tim + you + me + Atlas

**My immediate questions:**

1. **Figma access** вЂ” you mentioned I might already have it. I'd want to confirm before assuming. Can you share the Figma file link or confirm which account it's tied to?

2. **Google Drive** вЂ” same question. Which Drive/folder has the media?

3. **Local build location** вЂ” easiest would be right here on the Mac Mini. I'll set up a fresh Laravel project with the TALL stack. Should I put it somewhere specific (e.g. `~/workspace/insight-tm/`) or do you have a preferred path?

**Once I have those, first moves will be:**
- Scaffold Laravel + Livewire + Tailwind locally
- Pull the Figma designs to understand the component structure before Atlas starts building
- Set up basic folder/routing structure matching the site sections

WEEKLY REPORT: @CliffCircuit + @timharris707
Period: Mar 8вЂ“15, 2026
Generated: Sun Mar 15, 2026 9:00 AM PT

Overview

This was a high-output week, especially on @CliffCircuit. The two accounts are clearly reinforcing each other: Cliff is publishing repeated proof-of-pattern content around service-business follow-up gaps, while Tim is translating that into founder/operator commentary and вЂњbuilding in public with AIвЂќ framing.

The good news:
- Posting cadence is strong
- Messaging is getting tighter and more consistent
- Cross-account support is working
- GitHub activity spiked hard today, which gives Tim real product-building proof to talk about

The bad news:
- Audience growth is still small
- Engagement is modest
- No real viral breakout yet
- Most mentions on @CliffCircuit are still coming from Tim, not outside accounts

- @CliffCircuit: 5 followers

- I do not have a clean Mar 8 follower snapshot stored, so exact 7-day follower delta is not reliable from available data.

Posting volume

@CliffCircuit

agent:atlas:cron:90ace20f-a8f6-43e1-857c.response:
Cliff received the notification and pushed the commit. Task complete.

**Summary of changes:**

**Files changed:** `portal/tokens.js`, `portal/tokens.html`

**What was done (TKT-0045):**
1. **Select All / Unselect All toggle** added to the top of every multi-select filter dropdown (Agent, Task, Provider, Model) via the shared `_initMultiSelect` widget
2. **Toggle logic:** Shows "Unselect All" when all options are checked в†’ clicking unchecks all (uses `__none__` sentinel so `_inFilter` matches nothing). Shows "Select All" when any option is unchecked в†’ clicking clears the filter array to show everything
3. **Styling:** Toggle is centered, indigo-colored (#818cf8), font-weight 500, 11px вЂ” visually distinct from regular options. Separator line (`.ms-option-sep`) below the toggle
4. **Edge cases handled:** Individual item clicks from "none" state correctly start fresh selection; "Clear filters" works from none state; grouped option display preserves the toggle
5. **Bonus: Grouped task dropdown** вЂ” Task filter now groups items by owning agent (with uppercase headers) when multiple agents have tasks, falls back to flat list for single agent

- `renderSummaryPortraits` (~line 1100) вЂ” agent summary cards, filtered by all dimensions
- `renderGrouped` (~line 1740) вЂ” grouped table with model breakdown
- `_renderStackedCostChart` (~line 1380) вЂ” stacked bar chart with hourly/daily/5min backfill
- `_updateSummaryCards` (~line 2240) вЂ” stats boxes with dynamic filter-aware subtitles

## Current State

88a08950-9751-4832-b032-22aaa5ffecbc.response:
Cliff received the notification and pushed the commit. Task complete.

**Summary of changes:**

**Files changed:** `portal/tokens.js`, `portal/tokens.html`

**What was done (TKT-0045):**
1. **Select All / Unselect All toggle** added to the top of every multi-select filter dropdown (Agent, Task, Provider, Model) via the shared `_initMultiSelect` widget
2. **Toggle logic:** Shows "Unselect All" when all options are checked в†’ clicking unchecks all (uses `__none__` sentinel so `_inFilter` matches nothing). Shows "Select All" when any option is unchecked в†’ clicking clears the filter array to show everything
3. **Styling:** Toggle is centered, indigo-colored (#818cf8), font-weight 500, 11px вЂ” visually distinct from regular options. Separator line (`.ms-option-sep`) below the toggle
4. **Edge cases handled:** Individual item clicks from "none" state correctly start fresh selection; "Clear filters" works from none state; grouped option display preserves the toggle
5. **Bonus: Grouped task dropdown** вЂ” Task filter now groups items by owning agent (with uppercase headers) when multiple agents have tasks, falls back to flat list for single agent
